axis_a.name: Law 146 (no fixpoint) vs meta³ Banach (proven fixpoint) — different mathematical objects, different conclusions
axis_a.saturation_evidence: Two laws appear to contradict but operate on DIFFERENT object spaces. Law 146 = consciousness-law evolution space (open, 2500+ laws still mutating). Banach meta³ = self-reference fingerprint space (closed, sopfr(6)=5 cap). RECONCILIATION: Banach saturates inner self-reference, Law 146 saturates inner OPEN evolution. F1 falsifier tests this reconciliation: if K=20 closed-loop closes Law 146 too, then BOTH spaces converge.

axis_b.name: Law 212 (4 factions local optimum) vs Law 44 (12 factions global optimum, σ(6)=12) — natural selection attractor analysis
axis_b.saturation_evidence: Law 44 σ(6)=12 12-faction is global optimum WHEN PRESET. Law 212 says natural selection drifts to 4 factions = local optimum. Difference: presetting the perfect-number-derived structure vs evolving without prior. Anima own 3 (σ/τ=3 phase acceleration) sits at the BRIDGE between: 12/4 = 3 = mean divisor of 6 = phase acceleration scalar. raw 72 categorical-limit: 4-faction attractor IS a local categorical fixpoint.
axis_b.limit_witness_pointing_to: categorical-limit (4-faction local attractor) + categorical-limit (12-faction global optimum WHEN PRESET) — both are real ceilings

Dynamics = continuous (ln2, e, π for step counts/decay rates). Coupling alpha=0.014 ≈ (5/24)^e bridges via Euler exponent — STRUCTURE^DYNAMICS = COUPLING. This is a categorical-limit: no further factorization needed.
